title: Death Freeze: Show Loss Reason Until Key Press
content:
## Overview When a snake dies, the game now enters a **death freeze** instead of immediately jumping to the Game Over screen. The board stays visible and frozen, the fatal collision cell is highlighted in amber, a pulsing banner names the cause of death, and the game waits for any key press before running the normal end-game flow (high-score saving, leaderboard, Game Over screen). This applies to all modes: human single-player, bot-driven single-player, and bot-vs-bot. ## Problem Solved Previously, `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` were called immediately on death, which called `showScreen('game-over')` and set `display:none` on `#game`, hiding the canvas entirely. The fatal head cell was never even drawn — collision is checked before `this.snake.unshift(head)`, and `draw()` was never called on the death path. ## Architecture ### New Instance Fields (`src/main.ts`) Four fields added alongside the existing `deathCause` field (~line 154): | Field | Type | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `deathReason` | `'wall' \| 'self' \| 'opponent' \| 'head-to-head' \| 'starved'` | Banner-facing loss reason | | `deathCells` | `Array<{ x: number; y: number; who: 1 \| 2 }>` | Fatal head cell(s) to highlight | | `awaitingDeathAck` | `boolean` | True during the freeze | | `pendingFinalize` | `(() => void) \| null` | Deferred end-game callback | The existing `deathCause: 'collision' | 'starved'` field is **unchanged** — it still drives the Game Over title and winner nuance in `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer`. All four new fields are reset in `startNewGame` next to the existing `deathCause` reset (~line 1104), and `hideDeathBanner()` is called there too, so a restart never leaves a stuck banner or stale highlight cell. ### `beginDeathFreeze(finalize: () => void)` (`src/main.ts`) New method inserted before the end-game functions (~line 1801). Sequence: 1. Set `isGameOver = true`, `awaitingDeathAck = true`, `pendingFinalize = finalize`. 2. `stopAutoRotate()` and clear the game loop interval (same lines `endGame` runs — idempotent when `endGame` re-runs them at finalize time). 3. Hide `#starvation-warning` so it doesn't overlap the death banner. 4. Call `this.draw()` to render the frozen board with the death cell highlight. 5. Call `this.showDeathBanner()`. ### Four Death Sites Rewired (`src/main.ts`) Each site now sets the new fields and calls `beginDeathFreeze()` with the existing end-game function as a deferred callback, leaving all winner resolution, high-score saving, and leaderboard logic untouched. | Site | Location | `deathReason` logic | `deathCells` | Callback | |---|---|---|---|---| | Single-player collision | ~line 1161 | `inBounds(head) ? 'self' : 'wall'` | `[{x:head.x, y:head.y, who:1}]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Single-player starvation | ~line 1183 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Two-player collision | ~line 1231 | `headToHead ? 'head-to-head' : wall ? 'wall' : self ? 'self' : 'opponent'` | `head1` if p1Dead, `head2` if p2Dead | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Dead, p2Dead)` | | Two-player starvation | ~line 1275 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Starved, p2Starved)` | ### Death Cell Rendering in `draw()` (`src/main.ts`) Appended after the painter-sorted object-draw loop (~after line 628), active only when `awaitingDeathAck`. Each cell in `deathCells` is drawn with an amber/gold highlight palette (distinct from food-red `#ef4444`) **after** the main loop so it paints on top of any coincident body or opponent segment. **Bounds handling** branches on the grid rectangle (`x < 0 || x >= gridSize || y < 0 || y >= gridSize`): - **Off the grid rectangle (wall death):** the true cell projects mostly off-canvas. The highlight is drawn at the snake's last valid head (`snake[0]` / `snake2[0]`) **and** also at the true out-of-bounds coordinate so a sliver pokes past the border edge as an "off the edge" cue. - **Within the grid rectangle (self / opponent / head-to-head, or Iron Snake interior void):** projects on-canvas; drawn directly. Correctly shows Iron Snake deaths where the head entered the interior void. Starvation (`deathCells = []`) is a no-op here — the freeze shows the last live frame plus the banner only. Death cells are re-read on every `draw()` call, so the highlight persists across window resize and any redraws triggered while frozen. ### Acknowledgement Branch in `handleKeyPress` (`src/main.ts`) Inserted at the very top of `handleKeyPress`, after the INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT guard and **before** the Enter/r/Escape branches (~after line 752): ```typescript if (this.awaitingDeathAck) { if (e.repeat || e.key === 'Shift' || e.key === 'Control' || e.key === 'Alt' || e.key === 'Meta') return e.preventDefault() this.awaitingDeathAck = false this.hideDeathBanner() const finalize = this.pendingFinalize this.pendingFinalize = null finalize?.() return } ``` - Fires before Enter/r/Escape branches, so the first key during a freeze dismisses to Game Over rather than triggering restart or menu. - `e.repeat` and bare modifier keys are ignored to prevent a held or stray key from skipping the freeze instantly. - Camera control keys (rotate `q`/`e`, auto-rotate toggle, overhead `o`, perspective `[`/`]`) were initially whitelisted to fall through so the player could inspect the collision from any angle ("death cam"). The final implementation has **any key dismiss** the freeze — the simpler behavior. Resize and DOM redraws (not key presses) can be used to re-examine the board while frozen. - After `finalize()` runs, `showScreen('game-over')` fires, `awaitingDeathAck` is false, and normal Game Over key handling (Enter/r to restart) resumes unchanged. ### Banner DOM and CSS **`index.html`:** `
` added inside `.canvas-wrap` as a sibling after `#starvation-warning` (~line 97). Overlays the canvas without shifting layout; no `showScreen` needed (same trick as `togglePause`). **`src/style.css`:** `.death-banner` modeled on `.starvation-warning` (lines 184–206) — centered (`top: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%)`), larger, alarm-red, `pointer-events: none`, with its own pulse keyframe. **`showDeathBanner()` / `hideDeathBanner()`** added near `updateStarvationWarning` (~line 1750). `showDeathBanner()` builds text from `deathReason` (with per-snake P1/P2 labels for two-player) and appends " — Press any key". Banner copy: | `deathReason` | Text | |---|---| | `wall` | "Hit the wall!" | | `self` | "Ran into itself!" | | `opponent` | "Crashed into the other snake!" | | `head-to-head` | "Head-on collision!" | | `starved` | "Starved!" | ## Behavioral Notes - **High-score / leaderboard timing:** `saveHighScore`, `maybePromptForEntry`, and bvb `addEntry` fire at ack-time (key press), not at the death instant. Nothing depends on them firing earlier. Closing the tab during the freeze skips recording — acceptable. - **Both snakes die same tick (draw / head-to-head):** both fatal heads are pushed to `deathCells` and both are highlighted. The `head-to-head` reason is derived from the already-computed collision booleans. - **Iron Snake:** interior void cells are within the grid rectangle, so they project on-canvas and are highlighted directly. - **Starvation:** no cell is highlighted; the freeze shows the final live frame plus the banner. ## Files Modified - `src/main.ts` — new fields, resets, `beginDeathFreeze`, `showDeathBanner`/`hideDeathBanner`, four death sites, death-cell rendering in `draw()`, ack branch in `handleKeyPress`. - `index.html` — `#death-banner` element inside `.canvas-wrap`. - `src/style.css` — `.death-banner` styles and pulse keyframe.
